channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Venezuela ruling party fractures after Maduro capture
item.description: Chavismo loyalists are openly criticizing acting President Delcy Rodriguez for her warming relationship with Washington. The deportation of a former minister to the US and authorization for US military exercises in Caracas have exposed deep internal divisions.

item.title: Holocaust survivor Tomi Reichental dies aged 90
item.description: The Slovakian-born Holocaust survivor, who lost 35 family members in Bergen-Belsen and later became a prominent educator in Ireland, dedicated his life to sharing his testimony with future generations.

item.title: China condemns political suppression of Xinhua reporter in US
item.description: Beijing has accused Washington of political suppression following the expulsion of a Chinese state media reporter, marking the latest escalation in media disputes between the two powers.

item.title: Norway oil workers threaten strike from June 5
item.description: Nearly 8% of Norway oil sector workers have threatened to strike from June 5, potentially disrupting production from one of Europe largest oil and gas suppliers amid tight global energy markets.

item.title: Japan risks stagnation without early rate hike, ex-BOJ official warns
item.description: A former Bank of Japan policymaker has warned that Japan risks returning to economic stagnation if it does not implement an early interest rate hike, as the country navigates global monetary uncertainty.

item.title: European Cloud Providers Back EU Push to Cut US Tech Reliance
item.description: Thirteen European cloud providers joined EU lawmakers and NGOs to support the European Commission initiative to reduce dependence on US technology, amid growing transatlantic tensions.

item.title: Russia Bans Aviation Fuel Exports as Ukrainian Strikes on Refineries Continue
item.description: The Russian government banned aviation fuel exports until November 30, as Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure continue to impact domestic supply.

item.title: Myanmar Village Explosion Kills at Least 55 as Rebel Army Site Detonates
item.description: At least 55 people were killed when explosives stored by a rebel army detonated in a Myanmar village, with witnesses describing widespread devastation as search efforts continue.

item.title: Netanyahu Orders Israeli Military to Strike Hezbollah Stronghold in Beirut
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered the military to attack targets in Beirut southern suburbs, a Hezbollah stronghold known as Dahiyeh, as Israel deepens its Lebanon offensive.

item.title: China Coast Guard Patrols Waters East of Taiwan After Japan-Philippines Border Talks
item.description: China conducted law enforcement patrols east of Taiwan in response to Japan and the Philippines launching maritime border talks that overlap with areas claimed by China.

item.title: French Navy Intercepts Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker in Atlantic
item.description: France intercepted a sanctioned Russian oil tanker in the Atlantic Ocean, part of efforts to enforce sanctions on Moscows shadow fleet. President Macron announced the operation involved boarding the vessel Tagor.

item.title: Seven Injured in Iranian Missile Strike on US Base in Kuwait
item.description: An Iranian ballistic missile strike on a US air base in Kuwait injured four US service members and three civilian contractors, according to Anadolu Agency. The strike comes amid escalating US-Iran tensions.

item.title: China Tightens Outbound Investment Rules After Meta-Manus Deal Fallout
item.description: China unveiled new regulations tightening oversight of outbound investments by local companies and individuals, following controversy over the Meta-Manus AI agent deal. The rules aim to strengthen capital controls.

item.title: UK Bars US Commentators Cenk Uygur and Hasan Piker Over Israel Remarks
item.description: British authorities barred US commentators Cenk Uygur and Hasan Piker from entering the UK, citing public order concerns over their remarks on Israel. The Young Turks co-founder said he was deemed a risk to public order.

item.title: German Retail Sales Fall Less Than Expected in April
item.description: German retail sales decreased 0.3% month-on-month in April, beating analyst expectations of a 0.5% decline. The data suggests continued resilience in Europes largest economy despite global market uncertainty.

item.title: US Gasoline Market Faces Test After Near-Record Stock Draws
item.description: A near-record run of US gasoline inventory drawdowns signals shrinking buffers as seasonal demand peaks. The tightening supply comes amid ongoing Iran-related tensions affecting global energy markets.

item.title: Philippine Senator Faces Second Arrest Order Over Graft Scandal
item.description: A Philippine court ordered the arrest of Senator Jinggoy Estrada on plunder and graft charges, days after he posted bail for a separate case. The move escalates political tensions in Manila.

item.title: Markets Rise as US-Iran Exchange Fire But Keep Negotiating
item.description: Oil prices climbed as investors weighed renewed US-Iran military strikes against signs both sides remain engaged in negotiations. Markets view continued diplomacy positively despite escalation.

item.title: Right-Wing Outsider Leads Colombia Presidential Race
item.description: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombias presidential race, setting up a runoff that could shift the countrys political direction away from outgoing President Petros policies.

item.title: Five Killed in Explosion at Hanwha Aerospace Defense Plant in South Korea
item.description: An explosion at a Hanwha Aerospace factory in Daejeon killed five people and injured two others. The plant is a major facility for South Korea's defense industry, producing rockets and other military hardware.

item.title: Trump Says Iran 'Really Wants' a Deal as Both Sides Exchange Fresh Strikes
item.description: President Trump expressed optimism about reaching an agreement with Iran, saying Tehran 'really wants to make a deal' that would be good for the US and its allies. Meanwhile, both sides continued to exchange strikes, with Iran targeting a US base in Kuwait and the US hitting Iranian military sites.

item.title: US Proposes New Israel-Lebanon De-escalation Plan Amid Fighting
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke with leaders from both countries and proposed a plan for 'gradual de-escalation' as Israeli troops continue to push into southern Lebanon. The fighting has become the broadest spillover of the Iran war.

item.title: Pro-Trump Outsider Wins First Round of Colombian Presidential Election
item.description: Far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella won the first round of Colombia's presidential election with 43.7% of the vote. He will face leftist Ivan Cepeda in a runoff that could reshape Colombia's relations with the United States.

item.title: Nepal PM proposes dialogue to resolve India border dispute
item.description: Nepal's new PM Balendra Shah told Parliament both countries have encroached on each other's territories, calling for a joint study and friendly resolution of the Lipulekh, Limpiyadhura and Kalapani disputes.

item.title: US strikes Iran sites, Tehran targets US base in escalating exchanges
item.description: US Central Command said it struck Iranian air defenses and drone control sites after Iran shot down a US MQ-1 drone. Iran's IRGC said it targeted a US airbase used in attacks on southern Iran. Kuwait reported intercepting missiles and drones.
